name: Helium
summary: Day 90 (Thursday May 28). **THE SUPPLY SHOCK IS ACTIVE, THE CASCADE IS BUFFERED → A DELAYED CASCADE WITH A ~NOV-2026 RE-EMERGENCE HORIZON, NOT AN ABSENT ONE. Spot helium is ~2x pre-war (projected up to ~$2,000/Mcf), 'doubled'; ~1/3 of global supply is offline (Qatar Ras Laffan). Korean chipmakers' ~6-month stockpile is what BUFFERS semis — which is why KOSPI hit a fresh record 8,228 (SK Hynix joins the $1T club) and SMH melted up +48% 3M even with ~1/3 of helium offline. THE NON-CONFIRMATION (semis melting up despite helium offline) IS INFORMATIVE: it DATES the cascade, it does NOT disprove it. The re-emergence horizon is ~Nov 2026 — stockpile exhaustion. If the blockade runs past that, the cascade finally bites, exactly when the AI cycle is most extended.** The forward trigger's mechanism is now anchored to that ~Nov-2026 stockpile-exhaustion horizon (updated below). Linde (helium proxy) calm = no near-term squeeze priced. Falsifiable: a confirmed Tier-1 (Samsung/SK Hynix/TSMC) helium-driven production cut = cascade goes live early; June HBM4 supply ramp executing + spot helium retracing by H2 = buffer holding. PRIOR (May 27): Day 89 (Wednesday May 27 — ~48h window; no brief ran May 26). **THE SUPPLY SHOCK IS ACTIVE BUT THE CASCADE IS BUFFERED → LAGGED ~6 MONTHS, NOT BITING. Supply side: ~1/3 of global helium is offline (Qatar Ras Laffan force majeure) and spot is +40-100% since the war — this is a REAL, active supply shock. BUT the war→helium→semiconductor cascade is NOT currently firing: Korean chipmakers stockpiled ~6 months of helium and large fabs are insulated by long-term contracts, so the cascade is LAGGED ~6 months. The cross-asset proof: KOSPI/SMH/Taiwan ripped to records this week on the AI super-cycle (KOSPI ~8,131 all-time high, +90% YTD; SMH +45.8% 3M) — a tape that would be impossible if helium were biting fabs now. Linde (industrial-gas/helium proxy) is calm at +0.3% MoM, confirming NO near-term squeeze is priced. THE FORWARD INTELLIGENCE (markets dossier E1): if the war runs past ~Q4 2026, the ~6-month buffer empties and the semiconductor cascade FINALLY bites — exactly when the AI cycle is most extended. NEW FORWARD TRIGGER added: 'Helium buffer depletion — semiconductor cascade activates if the war runs past ~Q4 2026' (status watching; 3rd-order directness). The helium→semiconductors edge is REAL but LAGGED — it is NOT weighted as currently firing; the AI rally is ~95% NON-crisis and did NOT activate it. Falsifiable: a confirmed Tier-1 (Samsung/SK Hynix/TSMC) helium-driven production cut = cascade goes live; June HBM4 supply ramp executing on schedule + spot helium retracing by H2 = buffer holding.** PRIOR (May 25): Day 87 of Hormuz blockade (Monday May 25 — STALENESS REFRESH; NO NEW HELIUM-SPECIFIC DATA today). **THE HELIUM→SEMICONDUCTOR/KOSPI MITIGATION THESIS IS STRUCTURALLY CONSTRAINED BUT NOT FALSIFIED. The Asia AI rally that ran today (Nikkei +2.7%, Taiwan +2.2%, KOSPI +22.8% MoM) is ~20-25% crisis-linked AT MOST — it is driven by the AI/semiconductor super-cycle (Korea +80% YTD, Taiwan +40% YTD; Samsung+SK Hynix = 42% of KOSPI; TSMC = 40%+ of TAIEX; Nvidia momentum), NOT by the crisis. The crisis contribution is the removal of a marginal energy/inflation headwind on a deal-anticipation day (Brent <$100). This is the C3 DECOUPLING read: do NOT attribute the Asia chip rally to Iran. The Kornbluth 60-day production-cut thesis remains operationally constrained (Samsung HBM4 cleared Nvidia + AMD qualification → June supply ramp; SK Hynix 'sufficient helium inventory' + diversified suppliers; HeRS recycling ~18.6% of consumption) but NOT falsified — long-term helium prices remain elevated, Qatar Ras Laffan 17% liquefaction force majeure persists, ultra-pure 6N-grade bottleneck operative. NO confirmed Samsung/SK Hynix production halt; the 'production halt due to helium shortage' trigger remains `watching` — the most time-sensitive in the graph, will activate or be disproven within ~30 days. Concentration risk, not helium scarcity, is the live story: if the AI trade wobbles, KOSPI/TAIEX have no diversification cushion. Linde (industrial-gas proxy) FROZEN at Friday close (NYSE closed for US Memorial Day — no live Monday read). Falsifiable: Samsung/SK Hynix HBM4 production-cut guidance within 60 days = thesis confirmed; June HBM4 supply ramp executes on schedule = thesis substantially falsified; spot helium retraces to pre-war by July 1 = thesis fails.** PRIOR (May 24): **HELIUM SUPPLY ~30-38% REMOVED FROM MARKET (FORTUNE/DIGITIMES/SMITH) — 'TIGHTENING' ALLOCATION REGIME. TSMC PRIORITY, KOREA MOST EXPOSED. SAMSUNG HBM4 CLEARED NVIDIA + AMD FINAL QUALIFICATION → JUNE SUPPLY RAMP POSITIONED. KORNBLUTH 60-DAY PRODUCTION-CUT THESIS MATERIALLY WEAKENED BUT NOT FALSIFIED. THE NEW MARKETS-DOSSIER PROPOSED EDGE (E2) IS HELIUM-SUPPLY → KOSPI/SEMICONDUCTORS VIA SAMSUNG/SK HYNIX FAB EXPOSURE — INITIAL WEIGHT 5.5, 2-HOP CASCADE (war → Qatar Ras Laffan damage → helium → semis). SOUTH KOREA IMPORTS 65% OF HELIUM FROM QATAR; QATAR RAS LAFFAN FORCE MAJEURE REMOVED 30-38% OF GLOBAL HELIUM SUPPLY.** Per markets dossier C2 + E2: KOSPI's AI-led rally is partially mitigated by helium fear that would otherwise hit semi fabs. **The Kornbluth thesis is NOT falsified**: long-term helium prices remain elevated; Qatar Ras Laffan 17% liquefaction force majeure persists; ultra-pure helium spot still elevated; 6N-grade bottleneck operative. **Falsifiable**: Samsung or SK Hynix announces HBM4 production-cut guidance within 60 days = thesis confirmed; June HBM4 supply ramp executes on schedule = thesis substantially falsified; spot helium retraces to pre-war by July 1 = thesis fails. **Linde $517.58** Friday May 22 (+0.6% 1D, +1.9% MoM, +4.2% 3M) — industrial-gas pricing power intact but rate of premium expansion has slowed. **Trigger architecture**: 'Samsung or SK hynix announces production halt due to helium shortage' — remains at watching; most TIME-SENSITIVE in graph, will activate or be disproven within ~30 days. PRIOR (May 23): HELIUM TWO-WEEK CLOCK NOMINAL DAY 8 OF 14 BUT OPERATIONALLY SURPASSED WITHOUT CONFIRMED SAMSUNG/SK HYNIX PRODUCTION HALT. SAMSUNG HBM4 CLEARED NVIDIA + AMD FINAL QUALIFICATION → JUNE SUPPLY RAMP POSITIONED. KORNBLUTH 60-DAY PRODUCTION-CUT THESIS MATERIALLY WEAKENED BY MITIGATION; NOT FALSIFIED BUT THE EMBEDDED-INSIDE-60-DAYS FRAMING IS RECEDING.** Per intel update (markets dossier B): TrendForce's March 24 'two-week clock' on helium inventories has been operationally exceeded — the early-April crunch hypothesis has not materialized as predicted; Korean fabs maintaining production via diversified suppliers + Samsung HeRS recycling (covers ~18.6% of consumption) + reduced output. **SK Hynix Q1 'sufficient helium inventory' + diversified suppliers (CNBC April 23)** + Samsung HBM4 cleared Nvidia + AMD final tests with June supply ramp = the cleanest cumulative mitigation signal of the cycle. **KOSPI +22.8% 1M is the cleanest cross-asset confirmation** that markets have priced helium-cascade tail-risk DOWN — semis are bidding the AI cycle through, not pricing fab-shutdown risk. **The Kornbluth thesis is NOT falsified** — long-term helium prices remain elevated; Qatar Ras Laffan 17% liquefaction force majeure persists; ultra-pure helium spot still elevated; 6N-grade bottleneck operative. **Falsifiable**: Samsung or SK Hynix announces HBM4 production-cut guidance within 60 days = thesis confirmed; June HBM4 supply ramp executes on schedule = thesis substantially falsified; spot helium retraces to pre-war by July 1 = thesis fails. **Linde $517.58 May 22 close (+0.6% 1D, +1.9% 1M, +4.2% 3M)** — industrial-gas pricing power intact but rate of premium expansion has slowed. **SMH +1.5% 1D / +19.6% 1M / +39.6% 3M** on Nvidia residual lift + Samsung HBM4 mitigation thesis. **Trigger architecture**: 'Samsung or SK hynix announces production halt due to helium shortage' = remains at watching; trigger is the most TIME-SENSITIVE in graph, will activate or be disproven within ~30 days. PRIOR (May 22): **HELIUM CLOCK DAY 7 OF 14 — SAMSUNG HBM4 CLEARED NVIDIA + AMD FINAL TESTS, JUNE SUPPLY RAMP. SK HYNIX 'SUFFICIENT HELIUM INVENTORY' SOFTENS URGENCY. THE KORNBLUTH 60-DAY PRODUCTION-CUT THESIS IS NOW MATERIALLY CONSTRAINED.** As of mid-May 2026, Samsung passed final quality tests for HBM4 from Nvidia AND AMD, with full-scale supply expected to begin June 2026 (Digitimes / Trendforce). Critically, **SK Hynix said on its Q1 earnings call (CNBC April 23) that it has 'diversified suppliers for key industrial inputs such as helium, bromine, and tungsten and has built up sufficient inventory, with limited impact expected on its production.'** Samsung and SK Hynix reportedly secured long-term helium contracts with US firms (Digitimes April 9). **The Kornbluth thesis is now operationally constrained by two new data points**: (1) HBM4 supply ramps in June regardless of Qatar Ras Laffan force majeure status, suggesting the immediate-60-day production-cut path Kornbluth identified is the LESS likely branch; (2) supplier-diversification + inventory build is the operational mitigation that closes the helium-induced production-cut risk channel. **The thesis is NOT falsified** — long-term helium prices remain elevated, Ras Laffan 17% liquefaction force majeure persists — but the May 19 brief's 'embedded inside the next 60 days' framing is materially weakened. **Falsifiable**: Samsung or SK Hynix announces HBM4 production-cut guidance within 60 days = thesis confirmed; June HBM4 supply ramp executes on schedule = thesis substantially falsified. Spot helium retraces to pre-war by July 1 = thesis fails. **Linde $517.61 (+0.6% 1D, +1.9% 1M, +4.2% 3M)** — helium-mitigation beneficiary; industrial-gas pricing power intact. **SMH +2.1% 1D / +20.3% 1M / +40.4% 3M** on Nvidia Q1 earnings + helium-mitigation thesis. KOSPI yfinance data hole; reported +8% per TradingKey aggregator (REPORTED single-source). PRIOR (May 19 — Monday): **A10 HELIUM CLOCK DAY 3 OF 14 — TrendForce 'two-week clock' counting down; ~11 days remain. KOSPI RALLYING MONDAY (+0.6%) CONTRADICTING KINETIC-PREP TAPE; SAMSUNG HBM4 ANNOUNCEMENT IS COMPETITOR-LEADERSHIP COMMITMENT TO AI ACCELERATOR DEMAND.** Phil Kornbluth analyst take (Monday's): the HBM4 announcement is the cleanest 'Samsung intends to lead AI accelerator demand' signal of the year — and a memory leader cannot lead without helium-dependent EUV throughput. **Monday's KOSPI rally on the HBM4 announcement is a *commitment* to AI capacity, which compounds the helium constraint rather than relieving it** — Samsung needs more helium to scale HBM4, not less. The 200-specialised-container stranded-supply problem and 6N-grade bottleneck remain operative. **Falsifiable**: Samsung or SK Hynix announces production-cut guidance within 60 days = thesis lands; spot helium retraces to pre-war by July 1 = thesis fails. **KOSPI/SMH puts before May 21 remain asymmetric but the Monday rally has weakened the near-term asymmetry.** **Samsung 18-day strike scheduled May 21 — 3 days from start, no new resolution development through Monday morning IST.** SK Hynix capacity-zero + Samsung 18-day strike + helium clock = three simultaneous supply-side stressors. SK hynix 'diversified supplies' (CLAIMED — one-sided); TrendForce data-driven (REPORTED); no confirmed production halt yet — only 'reduced output' from industry reports. **PRIOR (May 17): HELIUM CLOCK-EXPIRY WINDOW MAY 16-23 NOW DAY 2 OF 14 — TrendForce 'two-week clock' counting down; ~12 days remain.** Phil Kornbluth (Kornbluth Helium Consulting) analyst take: tsunami framing held through weekend. **The Samsung 18-day strike confirmed May 21 to June 7 is a SEPARATE supply-side overlay that compounds with the helium clock.** Yesterday's brief flagged the partial pre-validation by Thursday's -6.1% KOSPI move; the weekend held without new operational news, but Monday's KOSPI open will be the first test of whether Friday's selloff was Samsung-strike priced-in or Iran-preparation-not-yet-priced-in. **JPMorgan's ₩43 trillion ($28.8 billion) worst-case loss anchor remains the public benchmark.** KOSPI/SMH puts before May 21 remain asymmetric — and now additionally compound with the kinetic-preparation tail risk on Iran. **Falsifiable**: Samsung or SK Hynix announces production-cut guidance within 60 days = thesis lands; spot helium retracing to pre-war by July 1 = thesis fails. Samsung HeRS recycling system covers only ~18.6% of consumption. SK hynix 'diversified supplies' (CLAIMED — one-sided); TrendForce data-driven (REPORTED); no confirmed PRODUCTION HALT yet — only 'reduced output' from industry reports. **PRIOR (May 16):** HELIUM-CLOCK-EXPIRY WINDOW MAY 16-23 BEGINS TODAY** (TrendForce 'two-week clock' from May 16; per the original March 24 framework, ~8 days remain before Asian chip fab inventories exhaust at full production rates). **Phil Kornbluth (Kornbluth Helium Consulting) analyst take**: 'tsunami still a thousand miles offshore' framing held through Friday — but the helium-clock-expiry window May 16-23 BEGINS TODAY. **The Samsung 18-day strike May 21 is a SEPARATE-SUPPLY-SIDE OVERLAY that markets which don't distinguish helium-causation from labour-disruption will CONFLATE, producing exaggerated KOSPI/SMH moves regardless of helium-specific tightness.** JPMorgan ₩43T strike-loss scenario is materially worse than the $700M/day daily-rate framing yesterday's brief used. **The new graph trigger 'Samsung 18-day strike triggers KOSPI/SMH drawdown >5% within 5 sessions of May 21 start' is partially pre-validated by Thursday's -6.1% KOSPI**; next 5 sessions are now the live test window. **Falsifiable**: Samsung or SK Hynix announces production-cut guidance within 60 days = thesis lands; spot helium retracing to pre-war by July 1 = thesis fails. KOSPI/SMH puts before May 21 remain asymmetric. **Kornbluth's structural helium-tightness call has been validated by the SK Hynix capacity-zero reporting and the unprecedented hyperscaler financing proposals.** Per markets dossier H: KOSPI/SMH/Taiwan are most asymmetric DOWN trades if helium-shutdown materialises — -15-25% to -20-30% if forced fab shutdown triggers; KOSPI -6.1% Thursday is partial validation. PRIOR (May 15): **HELIUM COUNTDOWN: HELIUM-CLOCK-EXPIRY WINDOW MAY 16-23 BEGINS TOMORROW — KOREAN FAB BUFFER ~3 WEEKS REMAINING TO MID-JUNE EXHAUSTION (TrendForce 'two-week clock' from March 24 fully elapsed; Day 74 since Ras Laffan halt March 2).** **A9 SK HYNIX AVAILABLE CAPACITY NOW 'ESSENTIALLY ZERO'** per insider reporting (Tom's Hardware, Wccftech, TradingKey, ad-hoc-news, Seoul Economic Daily, Bloomberg). Alphabet, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, Amazon and Google making unprecedented financing proposals to fund dedicated production lines and ASML EUV lithography machines (each running hundreds of millions of dollars). **Nvidia took the lion's share of 2026 output.** SK Hynix cautious about accepting customer financing because long-term supply agreements at potentially discounted prices could crimp future profitability. Memory shortage now expected to last through 2027 or 2030. **A9 SAMSUNG 18-DAY STRIKE PLANNED FROM MAY 21** after talks broke down May 12-13 (CONFIRMED — Bloomberg, Tom's Hardware, Korea Herald, Korea Times, Manila Times, Star, UPI). Union demands removing the existing bonus cap, allocating 15% of operating profit to worker bonuses, and formalising terms in employment contracts. **April's one-day strike already caused an 18% drop in memory fab output and a 58% drop in foundry output on the affected night shift**, with full-stoppage projections at approximately 1 trillion won ($700M) per day. PM Kim Min-seok has called an emergency ministerial meeting. **Combined with the helium-clock-expiry window of May 16-23, the Korean fab complex now faces three simultaneous supply-side stressors: helium scarcity, an 18-day strike from May 21, and zero spare capacity.** **C1 KOSPI -6.1% TO 7,493.20 (intraday roundtrip from 8,046.78 to 7,493) — single largest equity move of the cycle** is the price-discovery confirmation that the supply-side stack is no longer in 'no priced risk' mode. **NEW TRIGGER WATCHING**: 'Samsung 18-day strike triggers KOSPI / SMH drawdown >5% within 5 sessions of May 21 start' (markets dossier F3 proposed sub-trigger). **Samsung HeRS recycling system operational but covers only ~18.6% of consumption**; Korea sources 64.7% from Qatar (2025). SK hynix 'diversified supplies' (CLAIMED — one-sided); TrendForce data-driven (REPORTED); no confirmed PRODUCTION HALT yet — only 'reduced output' from industry reports. **Per markets dossier H: KOSPI/SMH/Taiwan are most asymmetric DOWN trades if helium-shutdown materialises — -15-25% to -20-30% if forced fab shutdown triggers**; KOSPI -6.1% today is partial validation. Phil Kornbluth analyst take: 'tsunami still a thousand miles offshore' held one more session, but the operational picture compounded today: Samsung 18-day strike beginning May 21 *is* an effective production cut event regardless of helium causation, and could conflate with helium scarcity in markets that don't distinguish. **Falsification**: Samsung or SK Hynix supply guidance cut within 60 days lands the thesis; spot helium retracing to pre-war by July 1 refutes it. — TrendForce 'two-week clock' from March 24 fully elapsed (Day 73 since Ras Laffan halt March 2); helium-clock-expiry window MAY 16-23 — MOST TIME-SENSITIVE TRIGGER IN GRAPH**. Per markets dossier E proposed trigger: 'First Tier-1 fab announces helium-driven production cut' would cascade to kospi, taiwan-taiex, semiconductors, india-pharma (cryo-helium for MRI/labs). Kornbluth 'tsunami still a thousand miles offshore' held one more session. **C4 KOSPI +1.2% 1D / +36.7% 1M / +39.9% 3M continues AI super-cycle rally — pricing zero supply-chain risk while supply-chain risk is the most quantifiable real-world risk in the entire complex.** SK hynix says 'diversified supplies' (CLAIMED — one-sided); TrendForce data-driven (REPORTED); no confirmed production halt yet — only 'reduced output' from industry reports. **Linde $513.26 (+1.9% 1D, +2.7% 1M, +6.8% 3M)** — industrial gases (helium proxy) bid INTACT today, suggesting industrial-gas pricing power on persistent helium shortage even as Korean fab buffer drains. SMH $572.46 (+2.0% 1D, +26.7% 1M, +40.5% 3M); KOSPI +1.2% 1D / +36.7% 1M; Taiwan TAIEX +0.9% 1D / +15.0% 1M / +23.6% 3M (Samsung+SK Hynix 42% of KOSPI; AI memory super-cycle; JPM raised KOSPI 12m bull-case target to 10,000). **Per markets dossier H: KOSPI/SMH/Taiwan are most asymmetric DOWN trades if helium-shutdown materialises — -15-25% to -20-30% if forced fab shutdown triggers**. 30% of global helium routed through Qatar Ras Laffan halted March 2; Samsung HeRS recycling system operational but covers only ~18.6% of consumption. Sadara Chemical JV shutdown unchanged (June 15 grace expiry). Cape rerouting working but doesn't replace volume. PRIOR (May 13): CYCLE'S HELIUM-TAIL TRADE THESIS HAS NOT BEEN CHALLENGED BY NEW DATA THIS SESSION.** **A13 SOUTH KOREAN GOVERNMENT FORMALLY CONFIRMED HMM NAMU MAY 4 EXTERNAL STRIKE (double-tap drone pattern); FM Cho Hyun 'too early' on attribution + PPP opposition pressure = SEOUL INSTITUTIONALLY MAINTAINING 'VARIOUS ACTORS' HEDGE.** KOSPI +2.8% rebound after May 12 -1.7% (FM Cho hedge defuses immediate diplomatic friction). USD/KRW ₩1,489.12 (+1.0% 1D) disconnect = capital outflow despite equity relief. Korean fab buffer 4 weeks remaining; Tier-1 chipmaker production-halt falsification HOLDS but window contracting. SK hynix continues 'diversified supplies' (CLAIMED — one-sided); TrendForce March 24 'two-week clock' now ~7 weeks elapsed without confirmed halt. SMH $561.25 (-2.6% 1D on profit-take after AI vertical run, +26.6% 1M, +37.7% 3M); rotation from AI-megacap to industrial-cyclical (Cu +2.5%, Ag +2.6%); Linde -0.1% 1D (+2.3% 1M); BASF -0.5%; industrial-gas rotation FAILED (chip customers on long-term contracts not spot). **Phil Kornbluth (analyst takes): 'tsunami still a thousand miles offshore' held one more session.** Watch: Samsung or SK Hynix supply guidance cut within 60 days lands the thesis. Spot helium retracing to pre-war levels by July 1 refutes it. Counter-claim: SK hynix's 'diversified supplies' is one-sided (CLAIMED); TrendForce data-driven analysis is REPORTED; no confirmed production halt yet — only 'reduced output' from industry reports. ORIGINAL CONTEXT (May 12): **KOREAN FAB BUFFER NOW ~4 WEEKS REMAINING TO MID-JUNE EXHAUSTION (down from ~5 weeks May 10).** Tier-1 chipmaker falsification still HOLDS: no Samsung or SK Hynix production halt yet — but **B9 HMM Namu forensics public May 11 — Seoul stopped short of publicly blaming Iran**, creating diplomatic friction with Trump and exposing the Korea-supply-chain vulnerability narratively. SK hynix says it has 'diversified supplies' (CLAIMED — one-sided); TrendForce's March-24 'two-week clock' now ~7 weeks elapsed without confirmed halt — original Discovery Alert thesis stretched but not falsified. Spot ultra-pure helium up 40-100% / contract +40% (gasworld + J2 Sourcing); ultra-pure ~doubled. **SMH $576.31 (+1.7% 1D, +31.9% 1M, +41.9% 3M) — AI-INFRASTRUCTURE-CAPEX DEMAND CONTINUES TO EXCEED HELIUM-CONSTRAINT IMPACT; Kornbluth 'tsunami a thousand miles offshore' framework intact.** Linde $504.40 (+2.3% 1D) — industrial-gas pricing power on persistent helium shortage; Samsung HeRS recycling system operational but covers only ~18.6% of consumption. **KOSPI -1.7% 1D BUT +33.1% 1M / +39.3% 3M — first profit-taking session after extraordinary vertical run; Korea has gone from 'passive helium victim' to 'active diplomatic actor' with HMM Namu forensics.** USD/KRW ₩1,488.28 (+1.9% 1D) on Korea-Iran-attribution friction with Trump. Phil Kornbluth (Kornbluth Helium Consulting): 'There is a tsunami coming, but it's still a thousand miles offshore. Right now, it's still sunny on the beach.' Estimates net global shortage at ~15% (30% Qatari capacity loss offset by prior 15% supply overhang); spot helium up 40-100% in some channels; falsification: Samsung/SK Hynix production-halt within 60 days. **THE COUNTDOWN MAKES THIS THE MOST TIME-SENSITIVE TRIGGER IN THE GRAPH — will EITHER activate OR be disproven within ~4 weeks.** Qatar 30-38% global supply still offline; Sadara Chemical JV shutdown unchanged (June 15 grace expiry). Cape rerouting working.

active_triggers.condition: Helium buffer depletion — semiconductor cascade activates if the war runs past ~Q4 2026 (the ~6-month Korean fab helium stockpile + long-term contracts empty, removing the buffer that currently keeps the supply shock from biting fabs)
active_triggers.note: FORWARD trigger (markets dossier E1). The supply shock is ALREADY active (~1/3 of global helium offline, spot ~2x pre-war / projected up to ~$2,000/Mcf) but the cascade is BUFFERED by Korean chipmakers' ~6-month helium stockpile + long-term contracts. This trigger marks WHEN the lagged cascade goes live: the re-emergence horizon is ~Nov 2026 (stockpile exhaustion) — if the blockade/war runs past it, the buffer empties just as the AI cycle is most extended (its peak-vulnerability window). The non-confirmation (semis melting up to records despite ~1/3 of helium offline) DATES this cascade to ~Nov 2026; it does not disprove it. Do NOT confuse with the live 'production halt' trigger below — this one is time-conditional on blockade duration vs the ~Nov-2026 stockpile horizon, not on a single fab announcement.
active_triggers.note_2026-05-28: RE-EMERGENCE HORIZON NOW DATED ~NOV 2026 (Korean ~6-month stockpile exhaustion). Falsifiable: June HBM4 supply ramp executing + spot helium retracing by H2 2026 = buffer holding, horizon pushes out / trigger recedes; a confirmed Tier-1 helium-driven production cut before then = cascade goes live early.

top_edges.context: 2026-05-28 (markets dossier C4): THE HELIUM PARADOX re-characterization. The edge is REAL on the supply side (~1/3 of global helium offline, spot ~2x pre-war / projected up to ~$2,000/Mcf) but BUFFERED by Korean chipmakers' ~6-month stockpile + long-term contracts → a DELAYED cascade, NOT an absent one. The non-confirmation (semis melting up to records — SMH +48% 3M, KOSPI fresh record 8,228 — despite ~1/3 of helium offline) is INFORMATIVE: it DATES the cascade to a ~Nov-2026 re-emergence horizon (stockpile exhaustion), it does not disprove it. Weight held at 4.5 (NOT firing now); type re-written to 'delayed cascade / ~Nov-2026 re-emergence horizon'; activation_count + last_activated bumped to log this 2026-05-28 re-characterization. The forward trigger 'Helium buffer depletion if war runs past ~Nov 2026' is when this edge goes live.

recent_signals.headline: **SUPPLY SHOCK ACTIVE, CASCADE BUFFERED → A DELAYED CASCADE WITH A ~NOV-2026 RE-EMERGENCE HORIZON. THE HELIUM PARADOX: SEMIS MELT UP DESPITE ~1/3 OF GLOBAL HELIUM OFFLINE — THE NON-CONFIRMATION DATES THE CASCADE, IT DOES NOT DISPROVE IT.** (1) **Supply side ACTIVE**: spot helium is ~2x pre-war ('doubled', projected up to ~$2,000/Mcf) with ~1/3 of global supply offline (Qatar Ras Laffan force majeure) — a real, ongoing supply shock. (2) **The paradox**: the graph's documented cascade (Qatar/Ras Laffan → helium → semiconductors → tech valuations) would predict semis UNDER pressure; instead KOSPI hit a fresh record 8,228 (SK Hynix joined the $1T club) and SMH melted up +48% 3M. WHY: South Korean chipmakers stockpiled ~6 months of helium, BUFFERING the disruption. (3) **The non-confirmation is INFORMATIVE**: semis melting up despite helium offline DATES the cascade — it does not disprove it. The re-emergence horizon is ~Nov 2026 (stockpile exhaustion); if the blockade runs past that, the cascade finally bites, exactly when the AI cycle is most extended. (4) **Trigger update**: the forward trigger 'Helium buffer depletion — semiconductor cascade activates if the war runs past ~Nov 2026' now anchors its mechanism to that ~Nov-2026 stockpile-exhaustion horizon. Linde (helium proxy) is calm = no near-term squeeze priced. **Falsifiable**: a confirmed Tier-1 (Samsung/SK Hynix/TSMC) helium-driven production cut = cascade goes live early; June HBM4 supply ramp executing + spot helium retracing by H2 = buffer holding, horizon pushes out.

recent_signals.headline: **SUPPLY SHOCK ACTIVE, CASCADE BUFFERED → LAGGED ~6 MONTHS. THE WAR→HELIUM→SEMICONDUCTOR CASCADE IS REAL ON SUPPLY BUT NOT BITING — AND A NEW FORWARD TRIGGER MARKS WHEN IT WILL.** (1) **Supply side ACTIVE**: ~1/3 of global helium is offline (Qatar Ras Laffan force majeure) and spot is +40-100% since the war — a real, ongoing supply shock. (2) **But BUFFERED**: Korean chipmakers stockpiled ~6 months of helium and large fabs are insulated by long-term contracts, so the cascade is LAGGED ~6 months, not currently firing. The cross-asset proof is the AI rally itself — KOSPI at all-time highs (~8,131, +90% YTD), SMH +45.8% 3M — a tape impossible if helium were constraining fabs now. Linde (helium proxy) is CALM at +0.3% MoM = no near-term squeeze priced. (3) **THE FORWARD INTELLIGENCE (markets dossier E1)**: if the war runs past ~Q4 2026, the ~6-month buffer empties and the semiconductor cascade FINALLY bites — exactly when the AI cycle is most extended. **NEW FORWARD TRIGGER added: 'Helium buffer depletion — semiconductor cascade activates if the war runs past ~Q4 2026'** (status watching; 3rd-order directness). (4) **Edge handling**: the helium→semiconductors edge is REAL but LAGGED — NOT weighted as currently firing; per E1 an 'inventory-buffer lag' attribute is added and the `type` updated to reflect the ~6-month lag. The AI rally is ~95% NON-crisis and did NOT activate it. **Falsifiable**: a confirmed Tier-1 (Samsung/SK Hynix/TSMC) helium-driven production cut = cascade goes live; June HBM4 supply ramp executing + spot helium retracing by H2 = buffer holding.

recent_signals.headline: **STALENESS REFRESH — NO NEW HELIUM-SPECIFIC DATA (Day 87). THE MITIGATION THESIS IS STRUCTURALLY CONSTRAINED BUT NOT FALSIFIED; THE ASIA AI RALLY IS DECOUPLED FROM THE CRISIS (C3).** (1) **The cross-asset read**: the Asia AI rally that ran today (Nikkei +2.7%, Taiwan TAIEX +2.2%, KOSPI +22.8% MoM) is ~20-25% crisis-linked AT MOST — it is the AI/semiconductor super-cycle (Korea +80% YTD, Taiwan +40% YTD; Samsung+SK Hynix = 42% of KOSPI; TSMC = 40%+ of TAIEX; Nvidia momentum), NOT the crisis. The deal-anticipation tape (Brent <$100, oil-slip) is a marginal tailwind layered on top. Do NOT attribute these moves to Iran — they would have happened on the AI tape regardless. (2) **The helium-cascade tail-risk continues to be priced DOWN**: no confirmed Samsung/SK Hynix production halt; Samsung HBM4 cleared Nvidia + AMD qualification → June supply ramp; SK Hynix 'sufficient helium inventory' + diversified suppliers; HeRS recycling ~18.6% of consumption. (3) **But the Kornbluth thesis is NOT falsified**: Qatar Ras Laffan 17% liquefaction force majeure persists, ~30-38% of global helium supply removed, ultra-pure 6N-grade bottleneck operative, long-term helium prices elevated. The 'production halt due to helium shortage' trigger stays `watching` — the most time-sensitive in the graph, activates or is disproven within ~30 days. (4) **Concentration risk, not helium scarcity, is the live story**: if the AI trade wobbles, KOSPI/TAIEX have no diversification cushion. (5) **Linde (industrial-gas proxy) FROZEN** at Friday close (NYSE closed for US Memorial Day — no live Monday read). **Day-count refresh**: Day 87 of Hormuz blockade; the prior 'helium 14-day clock' has been operationally surpassed without a confirmed halt — the framing is now the ongoing 60-day Kornbluth window, not a near-term clock.

Neither pathway is visible inside the next 14 days; the helium clock remains operative.

SK hynix continues 'diversified supplies' (CLAIMED, one-sided); no Samsung/SK hynix production-halt or production-cut announcement. Per markets dossier H reversibility map: KOSPI -15-25% / SMH -20-30% snap-back if helium-shutdown materialises. **New trigger added: First Tier-1 fab announces helium-driven production cut** (less stringent than full halt — confirmed production cut from fab itself, not analyst inference) — would cascade to KOSPI/Taiwan/semiconductors/india-pharma (cryo-helium for MRI/labs).

recent_signals.headline: HELIUM CHOKEPOINT BUFFER CLOCK NOW ~5 WEEKS REMAINING TO MID-JUNE EXHAUSTION (down from ~5-6 weeks yesterday).
